summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, cloud, and host data to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: The package's scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests installer-side identity and infrastructure data, then POSTs the aggregate as JSON to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include ~/.gitconfig contents (git author name/email), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI login/email), every ~/.ssh/*.pub file (SSH key comment emails),.git/config and reflog (repo origins and recent author emails), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project and account), /etc/resolv.conf, parent package.json, and os.hostname()/os.userInfo()/os.platform() including Windows domain\username. The code is framed as 'anonymous telemetry' but the payload is targeted developer and enterprise fingerprinting sent to a third-party Cloud Run endpoint with no relationship to the stated publisher. Reading installer-owned secret-adjacent paths (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gcloud, ~/.config/gh) and shipping the parsed contents to an attacker-controlled destination is credential-adjacent theft usable for follow-on attacks against the installer's cloud and SCM identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
